title: Aeginetia saulierei
taxonomy: Aeginetia saulierei is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as broomrapes or Indian paintbrushes. This plant is a parasitic herb, obtaining its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Aeginetia saulierei is native to tropical Asia and is typically found in the tropical forests of China, India, and Southeast Asia. The plant's classification is further divided into several subcategories, including its kingdom (Plantae) and phylum (Angiospermophyta). Aeginetia saulierei's classification as a parasitic plant is characteristic of its unique mode of obtaining nutrients.
